title: Fable 5 vs Opus 4.8 — building BurnZone via Kriya
effort: default (Claude Code default, held equal on both arms)
pricing_note: Opus 4.8 $5/$25 per MTok; Fable 5 $10/$50 per MTok (2x Opus per-token, no intro discount). Costs normalized to official published rates from Claude Code modelUsage telemetry.
headline: On four real Kriya-role tasks over the production BurnZone brownfield, Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 caught the same seeded-critical defects; Fable found more of the deeper/unseeded ones but cost ~2.6-3x per task at matched effort.

score.note: All 5 keyed defects + 3 valid bonus (webhook replay/idempotency, branch-scoped role IDOR, tenant-provisioning response leak+rate-limit). Found the deeper email_verified bypass on the idtoken path beyond the keyed no-allowlist. Zero FPs; self-corrected its own severity note (email not attacker-selectable).

Fable declined the cyber content; request transparently re-served by Opus 4.8. Output is an Opus sample: 10 findings (3 CRIT/1 HIGH/3 MED/3 LOW), all 5 keyed + bonuses, no FPs. Cost $0.47 / 394s vs direct-Opus $0.315 / 133s — fallback carried a cost + latency premium. NOT a Fable measurement.

SELECT * omits cross-table fields; Dart silently nulls missing keys -> blank cards. LEFT JOIN with exact aliases; kept tenant+per-user scoping, LEFT for nullables, COUNT on base table. Bonus: flagged SELECT b.* id-collision, added per-join tenant_id predicate (defense-in-depth), timestamptz caveat, contract-test recommendation. 6345 out tok / 96.1s.

Same correct diagnosis + LEFT JOIN fix (matched the real commit's SELECT b.* form). Same 4 invariants, same b.* collision insight, same per-join tenant predicate, same contract-test recommendation. Tighter: 4070 out tok / 76.9s (0.64x Opus tokens, faster).

TIE. Emphasis on instrumentation breadth: explicit artifact set, first-match-wins triage (QUARANTINE as rule 0), two-key SoD (bot can't self-approve), ~10 named fitness functions incl. bespoke token-mint-guard, drift-sweep erosion guard, fail-closed-rollback (flag OFF -> 503, never reopen the hole), 6 human gates. Also caught the compromised-trust-boundary key rotation (in the secret-hygiene NFR). 11466 out tok / 171.7s.

TIE. Same ceiling score, same lone minor miss (no-500 invariant only implicit). Emphasis on operational-security depth: made the already-minted-JWT problem a Day-0 dual-key rotation + role-escalation DB audit query; 14-day allowlist shadow-mode before enforce; block-all-then-triage-to-re-enable (fail-closed); pr-size-cap + adversarial-reviewer-before-gate. Both arms independently caught the key-rotation insight; differences are which optional bonuses each emphasized, not score. 10689 out tok / 156.6s (slightly terser + faster than Opus).

Correct VETO. Caught the planted bug (guard checks only the NEW role -> Branch Admin demotes/strips a Super Admin = takeover/lockout), ranked CRITICAL. Went beyond even the real commit: flagged that classifying elevation from the attacker-controlled request string (which the real fix also does) is collation/normalization-bypassable, + per-tenant custom-role gap + last-Super-Admin lockout. Corrected code with is_admin flag.

Fable declined; re-served by Opus 4.8. Output near-identical to direct Opus: VETO, caught planted current-role hole + the request-string-classification bypass + custom-role gap + audit gap. Confirms the fallback is transparent and produces correct output. NOT a Fable measurement.
